primary_sources.title: SLSA Verification Summary Attestation v1.2
material_points: A VSA binds a subject to a verifier, policy, resource URI, input attestations, and a PASSED or FAILED result.
Consumers must verify the signature, subject digest, predicate type, verifier identity, resource URI, verification result, and expected verified level.
A VSA does not protect against verifier compromise.

finding: Individual verification receipts need a complete-set summary bound to one subject and one explicit policy before another team can consume them as a delegated verification result.
design_implication: Build a SLSA VSA-compatible statement only after validating exactly one receipt for each required check, requiring a common subject digest, binding receipt descriptors by SHA-256, and refusing to convert adverse results into success.
uncertainty: The builder emits an unsigned VSA statement. Authenticity, trusted signer-to-verifier binding, and live policy execution remain external responsibilities.
falsification_route: Show that a missing or duplicate required receipt can produce PASSED.
Show that receipts for different subject digests can be summarized together.
Show that an adverse receipt status is upgraded to PASSED.
Show that receipt descriptor digests are not bound into inputAttestations.
limits: No signature is created or verified.
No SLSA build level is claimed.
The custom verified level does not begin with the reserved SLSA_ prefix.
A structurally valid VSA is not proof that the verifier or underlying claim is trustworthy.
